scholarships.name: Gates Scholarship (formerly Gates Millennium Scholars)
scholarships.description: Provides full college funding for outstanding minority students with financial need. Covers tuition, room, board, and other costs for 4 years at the college of the student's choice.
scholarships.eligibility: U.S. citizen or legal permanent resident. African American, American Indian/Alaska Native, Asian Pacific Islander American, or Hispanic American. Minimum 3.3 GPA. Pell Grant eligible. High school senior.

prompts.prompt: Describe your leadership abilities and experience.
Describe a significant challenge you've faced and how you overcame it.

scholarships.name: Coca-Cola Scholars Program
scholarships.description: Awards $20,000 to 150 exceptional high school seniors who demonstrate outstanding leadership and service in their communities.
scholarships.eligibility: U.S. citizen or legal permanent resident. Current high school senior. Minimum 3.0 GPA. Attend school in the U.S.

prompts.prompt: Describe the most significant community service you have done and why it was meaningful to you.

scholarships.name: Jack Kent Cooke Foundation College Scholarship
scholarships.description: The largest private undergraduate scholarship in the U.S. awarded to high school seniors with exceptional academic ability and significant financial need.
scholarships.eligibility: High school senior. Minimum 3.5 GPA. Demonstrate significant financial need. Plan to enroll in an accredited 4-year college.

prompts.prompt: What extracurricular activities or paid/unpaid work have you found most meaningful, and why?

scholarships.name: Regeneron Science Talent Search
scholarships.description: America's oldest and most prestigious science and math competition for high school seniors. Top 40 finalists travel to Washington, D.C. to compete for prizes.
scholarships.eligibility: High school senior in the U.S. or U.S. territories. Must complete an independent research project in science, technology, engineering, or math.

scholarships.name: QuestBridge National College Match
scholarships.description: Links high-achieving, low-income students with the nation's best colleges. Matched scholars receive a full 4-year scholarship including room and board.
scholarships.eligibility: High school senior. U.S. citizen, permanent resident, or DACA recipient. Household income typically under $65,000. Exceptional academic achievement.

prompts.prompt: Describe your family's economic background and how it has shaped your perspective.
Describe an intellectual passion that has affected your academic career.

scholarships.name: Dell Scholars Program
scholarships.description: Provides $20,000 over six years plus a laptop and textbook credits to students who demonstrate need and potential to graduate from college.
scholarships.eligibility: U.S. citizen or permanent resident. Enrolled in or accepted to a Title IV accredited college. Minimum 2.4 GPA. Demonstrate financial need. Participated in AVID, College Advising Corps, or similar program.

prompts.prompt: Describe the challenges you've faced in pursuing your education and how you've overcome them.

scholarships.name: Society of Women Engineers Scholarship
scholarships.description: SWE awards millions of dollars in scholarships annually to women pursuing STEM degrees. Multiple scholarship types are available.
scholarships.eligibility: Identify as a woman or non-binary. Enrolled in an ABET-accredited engineering or computer science program. U.S. citizen or permanent resident.

prompts.prompt: Describe your interest in engineering or technology and how it has developed.

scholarships.name: Google Lime Scholarship
scholarships.description: Supports students with disabilities pursuing computer science, computer engineering, or closely related STEM degrees.
scholarships.eligibility: Have a visible or non-visible disability. Enrolled in a 4-year university in the U.S. or Canada. Pursuing a degree in computer science, computer engineering, or related STEM field. Minimum 3.0 GPA.

prompts.prompt: Describe the most significant technical project you have worked on.

scholarships.name: UNCF General Scholarship Fund
scholarships.description: UNCF offers hundreds of scholarship programs for African American students attending HBCU and other colleges.
scholarships.eligibility: African American student. Enrolled full-time at an accredited 2- or 4-year college. Minimum 2.5 GPA. Demonstrate unmet financial need.

prompts.prompt: Describe your educational and career goals.

scholarships.name: Hispanic Scholarship Fund
scholarships.description: Empowers Hispanic American students to succeed in higher education by providing scholarships, access to resources, and support.
scholarships.eligibility: Hispanic heritage. U.S. citizen, permanent resident, or DACA recipient. Enrolled in a full-time undergraduate or graduate program. Minimum 3.0 GPA for undergrad.

prompts.prompt: How do you plan to give back to the Hispanic community after completing your education?

scholarships.name: Thurgood Marshall College Fund Scholarship
scholarships.description: Supports students attending HBCU member schools with scholarships and career development opportunities.
scholarships.eligibility: Enrolled at an HBCU member school. Minimum 3.0 GPA. Demonstrate financial need.

scholarships.name: First Generation Foundation Scholarship
scholarships.description: Supports first-generation college students in completing their degrees with scholarship funding and mentorship.
scholarships.eligibility: First-generation college student (neither parent has a 4-year degree). Enrolled in a 2- or 4-year accredited college. Demonstrate financial need.

prompts.prompt: Describe what being a first-generation student means to you and your family.

scholarships.name: TheDream.US National Scholarship
scholarships.description: America's largest scholarship fund for DREAMers, providing funding to undocumented and DACA students to attend college.
scholarships.eligibility: DACA or TPS recipient. Graduated from a U.S. high school. Annual household income under $60,000. Minimum 2.5 GPA. First time attending college.

prompts.prompt: Describe your immigration experience and how it has shaped your goals.

scholarships.name: Prudential Spirit of Community Awards
scholarships.description: Honors middle and high school students who have demonstrated exemplary volunteer service in their communities.
scholarships.eligibility: Student in grades 5–12 enrolled in a U.S. school. Must demonstrate outstanding volunteer community service.

prompts.prompt: Describe your most significant volunteer activity and its impact on your community.

scholarships.name: AmeriCorps Education Award
scholarships.description: Earn an education award by completing a term of national service. Can be used to pay student loans or education costs.
scholarships.eligibility: U.S. citizen or national. Minimum age 17 (or 18 for some programs). Complete an approved AmeriCorps service term.

scholarships.name: Scholastic Art & Writing Awards
scholarships.description: The nation's longest-running and most prestigious recognition program for creative teens in art and writing. Gold medalists receive scholarships.
scholarships.eligibility: Students in grades 7–12 enrolled in U.S. schools. Original artwork or writing in one of 29 categories.

scholarships.name: American Film Institute Conservatory Fellowship
scholarships.description: Fellowships for students pursuing graduate education in filmmaking at one of the top film schools in the world.
scholarships.eligibility: Graduate student applicants for AFI Conservatory MFA programs. Portfolio review required.

scholarships.name: DECA Scholarship Program
scholarships.description: Multiple scholarships for DECA members who demonstrate excellence in business, marketing, and entrepreneurship.
scholarships.eligibility: Active DECA member. High school senior or current college student. Demonstrate academic achievement and DECA involvement.

prompts.prompt: Describe your leadership experience in DECA or business-related activities.

scholarships.name: American Nurses Foundation Scholarship
scholarships.description: Supports students pursuing a nursing degree with a range of scholarships for various specializations and career stages.
scholarships.eligibility: Currently enrolled in an accredited nursing program. Various scholarships have different GPA and enrollment requirements.

prompts.prompt: Why did you choose nursing as a career and what do you hope to contribute to the field?

scholarships.name: Army ROTC Scholarship
scholarships.description: Full tuition, fees, books, and a monthly stipend for students who participate in Army ROTC and commit to serve as Army officers.
scholarships.eligibility: U.S. citizen. 17–26 years old. Enrolled or accepted at a college with Army ROTC. Minimum 2.5 GPA. Physical fitness requirements.

scholarships.name: Harry S. Truman Scholarship
scholarships.description: For college juniors with outstanding leadership potential who plan to pursue careers in public service.
scholarships.eligibility: U.S. citizen or U.S. national. Junior-year college student. Minimum 3.0 GPA. Plan career in government, nonprofit, or public service.

prompts.prompt: Describe a public policy issue that is important to you and a legislative proposal you would offer to address it.

scholarships.name: NSF Graduate Research Fellowship Program (GRFP)
scholarships.description: The NSF GRFP recognizes and supports outstanding graduate students pursuing research-based master's and doctoral degrees in STEM fields.
scholarships.eligibility: U.S. citizen, national, or permanent resident. Graduate student (or senior undergrad) in STEM. Early in graduate career (typically first two years).

prompts.prompt: Personal Statement: Describe your personal, educational, and professional development.
Research Statement: Describe your proposed research and why it is significant.

scholarships.name: Fulbright U.S. Student Program
scholarships.description: Fully funded grants for U.S. citizens to study, conduct research, or teach English abroad for one academic year.
scholarships.eligibility: U.S. citizen. Bachelor's degree by start of grant period. Proposing study, research, or English teaching in a participating country.

prompts.prompt: Statement of Grant Purpose: Describe your proposed project.
Personal Statement: Describe your background and how this grant fits your goals.

scholarships.name: Niche $50,000 No Essay Scholarship
scholarships.description: A monthly no-essay scholarship. Create a free Niche account and complete your profile to enter automatically.
scholarships.eligibility: Open to U.S. high school and college students. No essay required — just a complete Niche profile.

scholarships.name: Bold.org Monthly Scholarships
scholarships.description: Bold.org offers dozens of scholarships every month — many with short prompts or no essay requirements. New scholarships added weekly.
scholarships.eligibility: Varies by scholarship. Create a Bold.org profile to see matching opportunities.

scholarships.name: Scholly Scholarships
scholarships.description: Curated scholarship matching platform with hundreds of opportunities for students at all levels.

scholarships.name: Phi Theta Kappa Transfer Scholarship
scholarships.description: PTK members receive substantial scholarship advantages at hundreds of transfer partner colleges. Over $38 million available annually.
scholarships.eligibility: Member of Phi Theta Kappa Honor Society. Transferring from a 2-year to a 4-year college.

scholarships.name: NCAA Division I Athletic Scholarship
scholarships.description: Athletic scholarships offered by NCAA Division I schools cover tuition, room, board, and books for qualifying student athletes.
scholarships.eligibility: Student athlete meeting NCAA eligibility requirements. Must be recruited by a Division I program.

scholarships.name: Asian Pacific American Labor Alliance Scholarship
scholarships.description: Supports Asian Pacific American students who demonstrate commitment to the labor movement and to their communities.
scholarships.eligibility: Asian Pacific American student. Accepted or enrolled in a college or university. Demonstrate activism and commitment to labor issues.

prompts.prompt: Describe your commitment to social justice and the labor movement.

scholarships.name: American Indian College Fund Scholarship
scholarships.description: The nation's largest source of funding for American Indian and Alaska Native higher education, providing thousands of scholarships annually.
scholarships.eligibility: American Indian or Alaska Native student. Enrolled or accepted at a tribal college or other accredited institution. Demonstrate financial need.

prompts.prompt: Describe your tribal affiliation and how your heritage has shaped your educational goals.

scholarships.name: Young Entrepreneur Foundation Scholarship
scholarships.description: Recognizes and rewards high school students who have entrepreneurial spirit and potential for business leadership.
scholarships.eligibility: High school student in the U.S. Demonstrate entrepreneurial spirit through activities, projects, or a business venture.

prompts.prompt: Describe a business idea you have or an entrepreneurial project you have started.
